summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on two variance requests (BZA 2025-006 and BZA 2025-007) filed by Eric Black and Kimberly Keenly for property at 1438 O'Bannonville Road. The first variance requested a shared 25-foot panhandle road frontage for two lots instead of the required 25 feet each, and the second requested a reduced lot size for the front lot since the panhandle area does not count toward the minimum 40,000 sq ft lot area in the R1 district. Both variances were unanimously approved, allowing the applicants to consolidate three existing parcels into two buildable lots.
decisions: Approved video minutes of May 19th meeting (4 yes, 1 abstain)
BZA 2025-006 variance approved unanimously - shared 25-foot panhandle road frontage for both lots
BZA 2025-007 variance approved unanimously - reduced lot size for lot 1 (35,785 sq ft vs 40,000 sq ft minimum)
action_items: Staff to send notice of action and BZA findings of fact sheet to applicants (Eric Black and Kimberly Keenly)
Staff to look into addressing/numbering for the two new parcels
Board members to recruit two alternate members for open positions
Zoning workshop scheduled at 6:00 PM (date not specified)
BZA application deadline of July 2nd for potential July 21st meeting cases
key_amounts: 40,000 square feet - minimum lot area required in R1 zoning district
43,560 square feet - proposed lot 1 total area including panhandle
35,785 square feet - proposed lot 1 area excluding panhandle (approximately 4,215 sq ft under minimum)
42,011 square feet - proposed lot 2 area (meets minimum)
2.01 acres - total area of three existing parcels
36,000 square feet - 10% relief threshold staff could have granted
notable_topics: Variance for shared 25-foot panhandle road frontage (BZA 2025-006)
Variance for reduced lot size in R1 district (BZA 2025-007)
Consolidation of three parcels into two buildable lots at 1438 O'Bannonville Road
Flag/panhandle lot regulations in R1 zoning district
Need for two alternate BZA board members
Upcoming chicken variance case for exceeding four-chicken limit on small lot
attendees: Kimberly Keenly (applicant, 8868 Longbow Place, Mainville, OH)
Eric Black (applicant, 8868 Longbow Place, Mainville, OH)
notes: Mr. Shaw abstained from the minutes approval vote, likely absent from the May 19th meeting. The applicants' three existing parcels are essentially landlocked with only 25 feet of road frontage, and a trailer sits on the lot in front of their property which they do not own. A potential chicken variance case was mentioned for the next meeting on July 21st. The staff noted they have authority to grant 10% relief from the code, which would have brought the threshold to 36,000 sq ft - close to but still above the applicant's 35,785 sq ft. Meeting adjourned at 7:08 PM.
title: BZA 6-16-25
